content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 2140A • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

January 13, 1977

A-77-01-026

Mr. Leigh C. Storey, President
Downey Democratic Club
11003 Pangborn Avenue
Downey, California 90241

Dear Mr. Storey:

Thank you for your letter in which you describe the activities
of the Downey Democratic Club and request advice regarding your
reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act.

In your letter you state that the Downey Democratic Club
is an independent political organization which is not controlled
by any candidate or committee. Further, you state that the
club raised a total of $703.84 and spent a total of $745.16
during calendar year 1976 to support five candidates. The
club supported three federal candidates and two state candidates.
When the club's total expenditures are apportioned among the
candidates, you state that $447.10 was spent to support federal
candidates and $298.06 was spent to support state candidates.

Based on the facts contained in your letter, the Downey
Democratic Club does not have reporting obligations under the
Political Reform Act because the club does not qualify as a
"committee" as defined by Government Code Section 82013(a)
or (b).

Such a committee must raise or spend $500 or more in a
calendar year to support or oppose candidates or ballot
measures in California. Receipts or expenditures to support
or oppose federal candidates are not covered by the Political
Reform Act.

Since the Downey Democratic Club did not raise or spend
$500 or more to support or oppose state or local candidates

SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-2406

/10

Mr. Leigh C. Storey
January 13, 1977
Page 2


or ballot measures, the club has not incurred a reporting
obligation under the Political Reform Act.

If you have additional questions or if I may be of
further assistance, please contact me.

Sincerely,

John C. Greenwood
Program Coordinator

JCG:lkh

DOWNEY

[IDC logo] Downey Democratic Club

John C. Greenwood
Program Coordinator
Fair Political Practices Commission
107 So. Broadway
Room 2140-A, State Building
Los Angeles, Ca. 90012

Dear Sir:

The Downey Democratic Club as an independent political organization
was not directly or indirectly controlled by any candidates or their
committees.

Having read your instructions in regard to the Political Reform Act
of 1974, we believed that we did not fall under any of your pre-
scribed requirements to file statements.

After contacting your office and receiving more information and
instructions we are sending you the statement of our expenditures
as requested by you for a conclusive ruling as to our obligations.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Respectfully,

Leigh C. Storey
President
Downey Democratic Club
11003 Pangborn Avenue
Downey, California 90241

LOS/rs

Following is a list of fund raising and expenditures made by the
Downey Democratic Club in behalf of various Democratic Candidates.

Monies raised:

8-18    Wine & Cheese Fund Raiser                    $541.62

10-24   Southern Style Lunch                    162.22
                        TOTAL MONIES RAISED    $703.84

Expenditures:

            Rent                                $400.00
            Telephones                          184.05
            Electricity                          86.25
            Misc. Supplies                       74.86
                        TOTAL EXPENDITURES     $745.16

(Deficit of $41.32 made up by Club dues)

                                            Federal      State
CANDIDATES:  Carter/Mondale      20%      149.04         0
             John Tunney         20%      149.03         0
             Ted Snyder          20%      149.03         0
             Betty Wilson        20%         0        149.03
             Bruce Young         20%         0        149.03
                                           $447.10      $298.06

sections.question: Does the Downey Democratic Club have reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act based on its activities raising $703.84 and spending $745.16 during calendar year 1976 to support five candidates (three federal and two state)?
sections.conclusion: Based on the facts contained in your letter, the Downey Democratic Club does not have reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act because the club does not qualify as a "committee" as defined by Government Code Section 82013(a) or (b). Such a committee must raise or spend $500 or more in a calendar year to support or oppose candidates or ballot measures in California. Receipts or expenditures to support or oppose federal candidates are not covered by the Political Reform Act. Since the Downey Democratic Club did not raise or spend $500 or more to support or oppose state or local candidates or ballot measures, the club has not incurred a reporting obligation under the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Downey Democratic Club, an independent political organization that raised $703.84 and spent $745.16 in 1976 to support federal and state candidates, must report under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Downey Democratic Club has no reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act because although it spent $745.16 total, only $298.06 was spent on state and local candidates, which falls below the $500 threshold for committee registration. Federal candidate expenditures ($447.10) are not counted under California's Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request letter from Downey Democratic Club (dated without full year visible), FPPC response letter dated January 13, 1977, and detailed financial statements. The response letter clearly states the FPPC's advice. High confidence in extraction due to clear conclusion section and supporting financial documentation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Downey Democratic Club have reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act based on its activities raising $703.84 and spending $745.16 during calendar year 1976 to support five candidates (three federal and two state)?

CONCLUSION: Based on the facts contained in your letter, the Downey Democratic Club does not have reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act because the club does not qualify as a "committee" as defined by Government Code Section 82013(a) or (b). Such a committee must raise or spend $500 or more in a calendar year to support or oppose candidates or ballot measures in California. Receipts or expenditures to support or oppose federal candidates are not covered by the Political Reform Act. Since the Downey Democratic Club did not raise or spend $500 or more to support or oppose state or local candidates or ballot measures, the club has not incurred a reporting obligation under the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised the Downey Democratic Club that it does not have reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act because its expenditures on state and local candidates ($298.06) did not meet the $500 minimum threshold, and federal candidate expenditures are not covered by the Act.
